item.title: 阿布扎比国家石油公司总裁兼阿联酋部长贾比尔赴日会见高市裕子
item.description: 阿布扎比国家石油公司首席执行官苏尔坦·贾比尔将于周四在东京会见日本首相高市早苗，此时中东战事正阻断流向亚洲的能源供应。【原文：Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. Chief Executive Officer Sultan Al Jaber will meet Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in Tokyo on Thursday as the war in the Middle East chokes energy flows to Asia.】

item.title: 恐慌指数显示，随着战争持续，交易员正为印度股市进一步下跌做准备
item.description: 交易员们正买入避险资产，以防范中东冲突引发的新一轮不确定性。【原文：Traders are buying protection against fresh uncertainty from the Middle East conflict.】

item.title: 卡尼不排除若中东冲突扩大将采取军事行动
item.description: 加拿大总理马克·卡尼表示，"绝不能完全排除"参与因伊朗战争引发的军事行动的可能性，必要时该国将支持其盟友。【原文：Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said “one can never categorically rule out” participation in military action resulting from the war in Iran, and the country will stand by its allies if necessary.】

item.title: 北京承诺采取措施支持钢铁行业，铁矿石价格上涨
item.description: 铁矿石价格上涨，因中国重申将解决其全球领先钢铁行业的产能过剩问题，并为来年设定了广受期待的经济增长目标。【原文：Iron ore rose as China renewed a pledge to tackle overcapacity in its world-leading steel sector and set a widely anticipated economic growth target for the year ahead.】

item.title: 美国承诺在朝鲜半岛维持强大军事力量
item.description: 美国军方表示，在当地媒体报道华盛顿正考虑将地区军事资产重新部署至中东以应对与伊朗冲突升级之际，美军仍致力于保卫韩国免受拥有核武器的朝鲜威胁。【原文：The US military said it remained committed to defending South Korea against the nuclear-armed North after local media reported that Washington was considering redeploying regional US military assets to the Middle East as conflict with Iran spirals.】

item.title: 中国稳健的债务管理计划在充裕流动性支撑下安抚债券市场
item.description: 中国2026年债券发行目标符合市场预期，缓解了债券交易员的担忧。充足的流动性及对政策宽松的预期增强为债券交易员提供了支撑。【原文：China’s 2026 debt issuance target is aligning with market expectations, easing concerns among bond traders who are supported by ample liquidity and growing bets on policy easing.】

item.title: Anthropic在人工智能安全争议后重启与五角大楼的谈判
item.description: 人类中心主义公司PBC首席执行官达里奥·阿莫代已重启与五角大楼的对话，商讨其人工智能模型在美国军方的应用方式。这为双方化解这场令硅谷震惊的纷争带来了希望。【原文：Anthropic PBC chief Dario Amodei has resumed discussions with the Pentagon about the way its AI models are used by the US military, raising the possibility that the two sides can resolve a feud that’s transfixed Silicon Valley.】

item.title: 坐拥1.6万公斤黄金的亿万富翁称金价将上涨
item.description: 印度富豪乔伊·阿卢卡斯打造了全球最大的家族珠宝企业之一。他认为，面对地缘政治和经济风险冲击的世界，未来几年金价将持续走高。【原文：Joy Alukkas, an Indian tycoon who built one of the world’s biggest family-owned jewelers, sees gold prices tracking higher over the coming years in response to a world hit by geopolitical and economic risks.】

item.title: 日本工会设定更雄心勃勃的薪资目标，日本央行密切关注
item.description: 日本最大的工会组织之一将要求雇主提高工资，涨幅将超过一年前的诉求，这表明该组织对经济前景持续保持信心。日本央行将在本月晚些时候召开政策会议前密切关注薪资走势。【原文：One of Japan’s largest labor union groups will ask employers for wage increases that exceed what it requested a year ago, signaling sustained confidence as the Bank of Japan monitors pay trends ahead of its policy meeting later this month.】

item.title: 高盛资产管理公司表示，私募信贷领域的不偿付率仍处于低位
item.description: 高盛资产管理公司全球私募信贷联席主管詹姆斯·雷诺兹表示，私募信贷领域仍具韧性，仅有少数借款人难以偿还贷款。【原文：Goldman Sachs Asset Management LP Global Co-Head of Private Credit James Reynolds said the private credit sector remains resilient, with few borrowers struggling to meet loan payments.】

item.title: 中国承诺通过因地制宜的政策应对房地产紧缩问题
item.description: 中国官员在一次关键的政治会议上承诺稳定房地产市场，但并未宣布一些经济学家认为扭转多年低迷态势所必需的重大政策。【原文：Chinese officials pledged to stabilize the real estate sector at a crucial political meeting, but stopped short of announcing major policies some economists think are needed to turn around a yearslong slump.】

item.title: Medline收购方支持者通过售股筹集31亿美元
item.description: Medline公司部分大股东通过出售股份筹集了31亿美元资金，借势这家医疗供应商自去年12月首次公开募股以来强劲的股价表现。【原文：Some of Medline Inc.’s biggest shareholders raised $3.1 billion in a share sale, capitalizing on the medical supplier’s strong trading since its initial public offering in December.】

item.title: 科技集团敦促特朗普取消人类中心论供应链风险标签
item.description: 代表谷歌母公司Alphabet和苹果等大型企业的科技团体正敦促特朗普总统重新考虑将Anthropic PBC列为国家安全风险的决定，认为此举将对整个行业产生有害的连锁反应。【原文：Tech groups representing major companies including Alphabet Inc.’s Google and Apple Inc. are urging President Donald Trump to reconsider designating Anthropic PBC a national security risk, arguing the move would cause detrimental ripple effects for the rest of the industry.】

item.title: 马斯克称法官偏见迫使他全额支付推特收购款
item.description: 埃隆·马斯克作证称，他2022年收购推特公司时被迫支付全额价格，原因是特拉华州法官在要求他完成这笔440亿美元交易的诉讼中对他存在"偏见"。【原文：Elon Musk testified he had to pay full price to buy Twitter Inc. in 2022 because the Delaware judge in the company’s lawsuit to make him consummate the $44 billion deal was “biased” against him.】

item.title: 菲律宾通胀加速，预计因伊朗战争持续攀升
item.description: 菲律宾通胀率连续第三个月攀升，在伊朗战争引发商品价格预期上涨之际，使该国经济陷入微妙境地。【原文：The Philippines’ inflation rate rose for the third month in a row, putting the economy in a delicate position ahead of the expected surge in prices of goods in the wake of the war in Iran.】

item.title: 雷森考虑庭外重组方案，债权人承诺注资
item.description: 陷入困境的巴西糖业和乙醇生产商Raízen SA表示，为寻求解决债务困境的方案，该公司可能启动庭外重组程序。【原文：Troubled Brazilian sugar and ethanol producer Raízen SA said it could go into an out-of-court restructuring process as it looks for a solution for its debt woes.】

item.title: 中国计划发行440亿美元债券以增强大型银行资本实力
item.description: 中国将发行特别主权债券为部分大型银行注资，此举标志着北京方面正加大力度，以巩固这个规模达69万亿美元的金融体系，使其在经济降温和市场波动中保持稳健。【原文：China will issue special sovereign bonds to recapitalize some of its largest banks, marking an expansion of Beijing’s efforts to fortify the nation’s $69 trillion financial system against a cooling economy and market volatility.】

item.title: 英国《金融时报》称，美国击沉伊朗船只给印度总理莫迪施加压力
item.description: 据彭博社经济分析称，美国在斯里兰卡海岸击沉伊朗军舰的事件，已动摇印度对中东战争的审慎应对策略，可能给新德里政府带来压力。【原文：The US sinking of an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka’s coast has unsettled India’s calibrated response to the war in the Middle East and could strain the government in New Delhi, according to Bloomberg Economics.】

item.title: 澳大利亚家庭支出反弹，澳联储考虑再度加息
item.description: 澳大利亚家庭支出在1月份出现反弹，表明消费者需求具有韧性，尽管前景因利率可能进一步上调以及中东冲突升级导致的能源成本上升而蒙上阴影。【原文：Australia’s household spending rebounded in January, signaling resilient consumer demand even as the outlook is clouded by the risk of further interest-rate increases and higher energy costs stemming from the escalating Middle East conflict.】

item.title: 印度借助太空初创企业发射"保镖"卫星
item.description: 知情人士透露，随着地缘政治紧张局势加剧，印度正加速推进太空资产保护工作，该国安全机构已要求私营初创企业研发所谓的"护卫卫星"。【原文：India’s security agencies have asked private startups to develop so-called bodyguard satellites as the country accelerates efforts to protect space assets at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions, according to people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: 伊朗袭击事件后，日本、韩国股市从市场暴跌中反弹
item.description: 受美国和以色列对伊朗发动袭击引发的市场暴跌影响，日本和韩国股市出现反弹，强劲的美国经济数据提振了市场情绪。【原文：Japanese and South Korean stocks rebounded from a market rout triggered by the US-Israeli attack on Iran, as strong US economic data boosted sentiment.】

item.title: 马来西亚经济韧性增强，加息预期升温
item.description: 固定收益投资者正为马来西亚利率上升布局，这与市场普遍预期利率将维持不变的预测相悖——该国经济增速已逼近该地区最快水平。【原文：Fixed-income investors are positioning for higher interest rates in Malaysia, defying forecasts for a hold as the economy grows at nearly the fastest pace in the region.】

item.title: 中国将财政赤字目标维持在创纪录高位以刺激经济
item.description: 中国将持续实施财政刺激政策以支持经济增长，因国内挑战持续困扰经济，且地缘政治风险不断上升。【原文：China will keep fiscal stimulus flowing to support growth as domestic challenges stalk the economy with geopolitical risks on the rise.】

item.title: 韩国股市反弹，芯片制造商股价上涨，此前遭遇创纪录抛售
item.description: 韩国股市在创纪录单日暴跌后反弹，因恐慌抛售重创的市场中，抄底者重新入场。【原文：South Korean equities bounced back after their biggest one-day slump on record, as bargain hunters stepped back into a market battered by panic selling.】

item.title: 中国设定1991年以来最低增长目标，旧模式显露疲态
item.description: 中国设定三十多年来最保守的增长目标，这默认了支撑该国四十年快速崛起的经济模式正显露疲态。【原文：China set its most modest growth target in more than three decades, in a tacit acknowledgment that the model powering the country’s rapid rise for four decades is showing strains.】

item.title: 卡尼呼吁加拿大、澳大利亚引领中等强国集团
item.description: 加拿大总理马克·卡尼在澳大利亚议会表示，加拿大和澳大利亚应凭借其作为稳定、可信赖的民主国家的声誉，领导能够抵御美国、中国及其他大国主导地位的联盟。【原文：Canada and Australia should use their reputations as stable, trustworthy democracies to lead coalitions that can resist domination by the US, China and other great powers, Prime Minister Mark Carney told Australia’s Parliament.】

item.title: 中国科技股暴跌6000亿美元，人工智能成本或加剧跌势
item.description: 中国科技巨头股的持续抛售潮尚未显露缓解迹象，投资者担忧激烈竞争中不断攀升的支出。【原文：The ongoing selloff in China’s megacap technology stocks shows little sign of easing, as investors fret over spiraling spending amid heated competition.】

item.title: 韩国总统押注股市或将适得其反
item.description: 李在明在去年六月总统选举的胜利征途中，力推重整韩国股市作为家庭财富的新引擎——这项举措截至上周仍看似进展得极为顺利。【原文：Lee Jae Myung championed a revamped South Korean stock market as a new builder of household wealth on his road to victory in the presidential election last June – an initiative that looked to be going fantastically well as of last week.】

item.title: 金价上涨，因中东战争推升对避险资产的需求
item.description: 随着美元走软，且中东战争进入第六天仍无解决迹象，金价持续攀升。【原文：Gold pushed higher, after the dollar declined and the war in the Middle East entered a sixth day with no sign of resolution.】

item.title: 中国设定1991年以来最低经济增长目标
item.description: 天安门广场，3月5日。【原文：Tiananmen Square on March 5.】
